answer:
\boxed{B}

The **University of Madras** (now known as **University of Chennai**) was the first in India to introduce **M.Lib.Sc. (Master of Library and Information Science)** and **M.Phil. (Master of Philosophy)** courses in the early 20th century, particularly in the 1940s–50s. While other universities later adopted these programs, Madras University pioneered formalized higher education in library science in India. This aligns with its historical role in advancing academic disciplines in the country.
